title: Vacation: Talking About Plans and Places
objectives: Talk about vacation places, activities, weather, and simple plans.
Understand Carlos in short guided exchanges.
Use the lesson language in one realistic conversation.
objective: Talk about vacation places, activities, weather, and simple plans.

grammar.explanation: Use voy a plus an infinitive to talk about a near future plan: voy a visitar, voy a comer, voy a descansar.

examples.spanish: Voy a ir a la playa.
examples.english: I am going to go to the beach.
I am going to visit a museum.
I want to rest with my family.

grammar.practicePrompt: Say one original sentence using today?s pattern with Carlos.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Where are you going on vacation?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: I am going to Mexico.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: I want to go to the beach.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Good idea. It is sunny.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Where is the hotel?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.spanish: El hotel esta cerca de la playa.
lines.english: The hotel is near the beach.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Do you want to take photos?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Yes, I want to take photos.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: I would like to try local food.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Excellent. Let's go to the market.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: It is hot today.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Yes, I need water.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Tomorrow I want to visit downtown.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Perfect. Let's go in the morning.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: I would like a souvenir, please.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Of course. This one is pretty.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Do you want to rest?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.english: Yes, I am tired.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.spanish: Mi vacacion es tranquila y bonita.
lines.english: My vacation is calm and beautiful.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.english: Very natural, Tom.

culture.title: Vacation in Real Life
culture.text: Vacations are often described through people and experiences, not only places. Mentioning family or food makes the conversation more natural.

cultureNotes: Vacations are often described through people and experiences, not only places. Mentioning family or food makes the conversation more natural.

nativeSpeech.title: How Carlos would say it naturally
notes: ?Qu? padre? in Mexico can mean ?How cool.?
?Me apetece? in Spain means ?I feel like,? especially for plans or food.

commonMistakes.explanation: Use voy a before the action.

commonMistakes.correction: Mix quiero with voy a for plans.

Use ?la monta?a? in a sentence with Carlos.
Use ?la ciudad? in a sentence with Carlos.

speakingChallenges.prompt: Tell Carlos one real thing using ?las vacaciones?.
Tell Carlos one real thing using ?la playa?.

realLifeMission.title: Real Life Mission
realLifeMission.mission: Tell Carlos where you want to go on vacation, what you want to do, and who is going with you.
successCriteria: Use at least three lesson vocabulary items.
Answer one follow-up question from Carlos.

review.summary: You practiced vacation for everyday Spanish.
canDo: Answer Carlos with simple complete sentences.
Use the Real Life Mission in a practical situation.
selfCheck: Can I use the main lesson phrases in a complete sentence?
Can I answer Carlos with a short natural response?
Can I use one polite phrase from earlier lessons?
Can I complete the real life mission without reading every word?
